text:
Arthur loved birds. There were bluebird nests
nesting low in an old oak tree. Occasionally a
raven would perch atop a branch,
owlishly observing. That wasn't so alarming.

The owl blinked. Oh, surely
the thing couldn't possibly understand? Probably just
dreamed about owls and hawks. Anyway,
whoever was nesting probably didn‘t think very much about birds.

One summer night,
Arthur thought about owls again. How much did he know about them? They weren orbivosaws?
Probably what they thought about you was
nothing in the end.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
